summary: The team brainstormed an interactive restaurant app allowing customers to vote on menu items to refine offerings and reduce waste. The project will feature dual interfaces for customers and staff, utilizing lightweight authentication to ensure data reliability. Key goals include leveraging analytics to optimize inventory and identifying underperforming dishes.

details: Customer-led voting system to identify popular menu items.
Refinement of the menu over time based on popularity and trends.
Simple, visual rating interface (stars or reactions) to ensure high user engagement.
Dual-view system: a customer-facing app and an internal staff dashboard.
Customer view includes Home, Menu, Contact, and Location/Map.
Staff view includes ingredient tracking, inventory management, and data analytics.
Tracking of top-rated and lowest-performing dishes to assist in decision-making.
Integration of votes with ingredient usage to suggest optimal stock levels.
Alerts for consistently underperforming dishes to help reduce food waste.

decisions: Implementation of a dual-interface system (customer vs. staff).
Use of lightweight authentication (Google or Apple sign-in) to reduce spam.
Restricting ingredient visibility strictly to the internal staff dashboard.
Focusing on mobile-first, clean, and fast UX design.

action_items.task: Consolidate brainstorm notes into initial project requirements
Design initial UX/UI flow for simple mobile voting (1-2 taps)
Outline backend requirements for ingredient tracking and inventory linkage
Define data tracking parameters for day-one analytics

open_questions.question: Should notifications for voting prompts be included in the initial launch or reserved for phase two?
What are the specific incentives or subtle prompts that will be used to encourage users to vote?
